response.justification: Bogdan meets the minimum bar on paper — roughly 19 years of software engineering experience and a Bachelor's in Computer Science from ETH Zürich — and his founder experience with GISCollective plus recent MCP/agentic-tooling work at re:cinq are genuine assets. However, this is a director-level principal role centered on AI strategy, and his track record is that of a strong senior/staff hands-on developer, not a recognized AI expert or strategic technical leader. His AI exposure began in 2025 and is tooling-level, with no demonstrated AI products at scale, no advanced AI degree, and no evidence of advising executives or strategic customers, making the level and domain gap too large for this particular role.
strengths: 19+ years of broad, polyglot engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform used in production
Recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks) plus a history of public talks and workshops
concerns: Operating level is senior/staff IC; no evidence of principal/director-level scope, strategy ownership, or advising strategic customers
AI experience is recent (since ~2025) and tooling-focused — falls well short of 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products or platforms'
No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field, and cloud experience is applied (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) rather than cloud platform strategy

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs director-level IC role
Limited depth and recency of AI expertise
Long tenure, founder experience, and builder fluency
